# Expected outputs (auto-generated)

**Generated by:** `scripts/sync_data_tables.py`.

If you reproduce this artifact end-to-end, the values below are what
your runs should match. Hashes are reachable-only CRC32 over the
distance vectors emitted by `--save-cert=<prefix>`.

## Verification counts you should see

- 1000/1000 SAT in `w3_stress.jsonl` (NVIDIA path) and `w3_amd_stress.jsonl` (AMD path)
- 12/12 SAT in `w3_task1.jsonl` (4 datasets × 3 algorithm paths)
- 48/48 SAT-when-verified in `e8_scaling.jsonl` (4 RMAT scales × 4 configs × 3 reps)
- 16/16 SAT in `e9_weights.jsonl` (4 weight dists × 2 graphs × 2 reps)
- 7 SAT + 2 expected UNSAT in `e1_nvidia.jsonl` (gaussian × road FP32 is the F10 boundary case)

## Findings F17-F24 quick-check counts

The findings folded into `paper_v3.md` correspond to specific cell counts. If your reproduction gives different totals, something is off — see `replication_recipes.md` for per-finding diagnostics.

| Finding | What | Expected count |
|---|---|---|
| **F17** | NV ↔ AMD `np.array_equal(d, d) == True` over full vector | 17/17 cert pairs |
| **F18** | Differing-π vertices that have ≥2 FP-tied incoming candidates | 100% of differing-π vertices (across the 5 differing pairs) |
| **F19** | Smallest-K-SAT on strict-mode corpus | 1 (= ε itself) on all 8 NV + 6 AMD configs |
| **F20** | Multi-source SAT cells (3 datasets × 3 sources × 2 vendors) | 18/18 SAT, AMD `d_hash` byte-identical to NV at every cell |
| **F21** | -O level SAT cells (3 -O levels × 2 datasets × 2 vendors) | 12/12 SAT, byte-identical across all -O levels and across vendors |
| **F22 + F23** | Gunrock `d` byte-identical to ours | 6/6 datasets (ny_road / web_google / livejournal / usa_road / RMAT-20 ef=32 / RMAT-22 ef=32 — FP32 packed atomic) |
| **F24** | Δ-stepping bucket-width sensitivity SAT cells | 18/18 strict cells (1 unique `d_hash` per cell, all SAT, byte-identical NV ↔ AMD); 18/18 relaxed cells (5 unique per cell, all UNSAT_RELAXATION) |

## d_hash quick reference for F24

Strict-mode `d_hash` values (independent of Δ on both vendors):

| Dataset | Strict `d_hash` |
|---|---|
| ny_road | `99f897ed` |
| web_google | `f0c9958f` |
| livejournal | `1cd2962d` |

If your strict-mode runs of F24 produce these hashes on every Δ ∈ {0.5×avg, 1.0×avg, 2.0×avg} on both NV and AMD, your kernel + atomic CAS path is byte-equivalent to our reference; the §5 boundary should reproduce as expected.

## Gunrock cell-by-cell hashes (F22 + F23)

Gunrock 2.2.0 (commit `748f79e`) `d_hash` (after FLT_MAX → +∞ normalization, identical to ours):

| Dataset | Gunrock `d_hash` (= ours) |
|---|---|
| ny_road | `99f897ed` |
| web_google | `330d7c0d` |
| livejournal | `5c0e3454` |
| usa_road | (full vector byte-equal; no separate hash kept in tables) |
| rmat-20 seed=42 ef=32 | (full vector byte-equal) |
| rmat-22 seed=42 ef=32 | (full vector byte-equal) |
